title: crotalaria novae-hollandiae subsp. crassipes
intro: Crotalaria novae-hollandiae subsp. crassipes, commonly known as the rough-pod rattlepod, is a plant species that belongs to the legume family. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Crotalaria novae-hollandiae subsp. crassipes include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use in various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures.
